commit.message:
Add system prompts for Claude 4.5 models: Haiku, Opus, and Sonnet

- Introduced new markdown files for Claude Haiku 4.5, Claude Opus 4.5, and Claude Sonnet 4.5.
- Updated product information, refusal handling, legal and financial advice, tone and formatting guidelines, and user wellbeing instructions.
- Ensured consistency across model prompts while addressing user interactions and safety protocols.

commit.message:
Rename and format Claude Code output styles to match repo convention

- Renamed files to include date suffix (_20251007) following repo naming pattern
- Added header with filename to each file (e.g., # claude-code-output-style-default_20251007)
- Added source attribution: "Extracted from Claude Code CLI (cli.js)"
- Format now consistent with other entries (like claude-artifacts_20240620.md)

Note: These files document Claude Code's output styles (agent configuration)
rather than model-specific prompts, so the naming follows the pattern
"claude-code-[feature]_[date].md" instead of the typical model-based naming.

These output styles are used in both Claude Code CLI and Claude Agent SDK
to modify the agent's system prompt and behavior.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add Claude Code's default output styles

Output styles directly modify Claude Code's system prompt and can also be used in Claude Agent SDK.

Anthropic explains how output styles work, but don't actually provide the output styles they use.

This is useful for anyone building agents with Anthropic's new Claude Agents SDK and want to know the actual output styles that Claude Code actually uses.

It was useful to me since I'm engineering a bunch of system prompts and having multiple agents that use the same output style is very useful, but without really knowing how Claude implements their own, I felt in a limbo.

I extracted them directly from Claude Code's cli.js.

Create cluely-05122025.md

readme_html:
leaked-system-prompts

Description

This repository is a collection of leaked system prompts from widely used LLM based services.

  1. If you would like to submit a PR, please match the format of other documents. You must include sources that I can verify or reproducible prompts.
  2. If the above process is too cumbersome, you can simply post a link in the Issues section. If there are verifiable sources or reproducible prompts, I will verify them and then proceed with the merge.
  3. This repository is cited in many papers. To prevent repository takedown due to DMCA warnings, please do not include sensitive commercial source code.
